question: Describe the flow of coolant between the steam generator and the reactor.

nuscale_ch01_0743.title: SRP 15.4.4-15.4.5, Rev 2: Startup of an Inactive Loop or Recirculation Loop at an Incorrect Temperature, and Flow Controller Malfunction Causing an Increase in BWR Core Flow RateII.C Events of Moderate Frequency
nuscale_ch01_0743.summary: This section explains that the regulatory guidance in SRP 15.4.4-15.4.5, which addresses startup of inactive or recirculation loops at incorrect temperatures and flow controller malfunctions in PWR and BWR designs, does not apply to the NuScale reactor because it does not use reactor coolant pumps or forced coolant flow. Instead, any relevant postulated startup reactivity accidents for NuScale are addressed under DSRS 15.4.6, which covers inadvertent decreases in boron concentration.

nuscale_ch01_0745.title: SRP 15.4.4-15.4.5, Rev 2: Startup of an Inactive Loop or Recirculation Loop at an Incorrect Temperature, and Flow Controller Malfunction Causing an Increase in BWR Core Flow RateII.E Single Failure
nuscale_ch01_0745.summary: This section explains that the regulatory guidance in SRP 15.4.4-15.4.5 regarding startup of an inactive loop or recirculation loop at an incorrect temperature, and flow controller malfunction causing increased BWR core flow, is not applicable to the NuScale reactor design. This is because NuScale does not use reactor coolant pumps or forced coolant flow, and relevant accident scenarios are addressed under different guidance (DSRS 15.4.6).

nuscale_ch01_0451.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsPSGD B.1 thru B.4Preheat Steam Generator Designs
nuscale_ch01_0451.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use top-feed, preheat, or once-through steam generator designs, so the specific regulatory guidelines for avoiding water hammers in these systems are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a passive natural circulation system (DHRS) that fulfills a similar safety function without the need for auxiliary feedwater pumps or the design features addressed by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0452.title: BTP 10-2, Rev 4: Design Guidelines for Avoiding Water Hammers in Steam GeneratorsOTSGD B.1 Once-Through Steam Generator Designs - Auxiliary Feedwater Supply
nuscale_ch01_0452.summary: This section explains that the NuScale plant design does not use the steam generator configurations (top-feed, preheat, or once-through with auxiliary feedwater) addressed by BTP 10-2, Rev 4, and therefore the associated water hammer guidelines are not applicable. Instead, NuScale uses a passive Decay Heat Removal System (DHRS) that fulfills a similar safety function without the need for auxiliary feedwater pumps or external supply headers, inherently avoiding the water hammer issues targeted by these guidelines.

nuscale_ch01_0748.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.2 Fuel Cladding Integrity
nuscale_ch01_0748.summary: The section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with regulatory criteria regarding inadvertent decreases in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system, specifically addressing fuel cladding integrity. It states that NuScale’s design meets the relevant requirements, noting that Critical Heat Flux (CHF) is the appropriate term for the phenomenon in their system, and that operator action is not required to mitigate such events.

nuscale_ch01_0747.title: SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2: Inadvertent Decrease in Boron Concentration in the Reactor Coolant System (PWR)II.1 Reactor Coolant and Main Steam System Pressures
nuscale_ch01_0747.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with SRP 15.4.6, Rev 2, regarding inadvertent decreases in boron concentration in the reactor coolant system for PWRs. It states that NuScale’s design meets the relevant regulatory criteria for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, fuel cladding integrity, incident frequency, and analysis methods, while noting that certain requirements (such as operator action and non-safety systems) are not applicable due to unique design features like the absence of reactor coolant pumps.

nuscale_ch01_0018.title: Principal Design Criteria
nuscale_ch01_0018.summary: The section describes the principal design criteria for the NuScale plant, focusing on the Site Cooling Water System (SCWS) and the overall facility process. It outlines the reactor core's heat transfer process, natural coolant circulation, steam generation, and the flow of superheated steam to the turbine and condenser, emphasizing the system's role in efficient heat removal and power generation.

nuscale_ch01_0044.title: Chemical and Volume Control System
nuscale_ch01_0044.summary: The Chemical and Volume Control System section describes the NuScale reactor’s passive decay heat removal system, which uses two independent trains, each capable of removing 100% of the decay heat via natural circulation and passive condensers in the reactor pool. In case of a steam generator tube failure or loss of AC power, the system isolates the affected components and passively transfers heat to a large, stainless steel-lined ultimate heat sink pool, ensuring continued cooling and safety without external power.

nuscale_ch01_0436.title: SRP 10.4.4, Rev 3: Turbine Bypass SystemII.3 MSIV Alternate Leakage Path
nuscale_ch01_0436.summary: This section summarizes NuScale’s conformance with various regulatory criteria related to turbine bypass and associated systems. Specifically, it notes that the requirement for an MSIV Alternate Leakage Path under SRP 10.4.4, Rev 3 is not applicable to NuScale, as it applies only to BWRs (Boiling Water Reactors), not to NuScale’s design. The document also details compliance or exemptions for other related systems and criteria.

edges.description: Section A and Section B both state that the regulatory guidance for startup of inactive loops and flow controller malfunctions is not applicable to the NuScale design because it does not use reactor coolant pumps or forced flow, directly connecting the coolant flow characteristics between the steam generator and reactor to the inapplicability of these requirements.

Section A and Section B both discuss the inapplicability of certain regulatory guidance for traditional PWRs to the NuScale design due to the absence of reactor coolant pumps and auxiliary feedwater pumps, which directly affects the flow of coolant between the steam generator and the reactor.

Both Section A and Section B explain why certain regulatory guidance related to coolant flow and auxiliary feedwater systems for traditional PWRs is not applicable to the NuScale design, specifically because NuScale does not use reactor coolant pumps or AFW pumps, thus connecting them through the unique features of NuScale's coolant flow between the steam generator and reactor.

Both Section A and Section B state that the referenced SRP guidance is not applicable to the NuScale design because it does not use reactor coolant pumps or forced coolant flow, directly connecting their regulatory interpretations based on the same design feature.

Section A and Section B both state that the NuScale design does not require or include reactor coolant pumps, referencing the inapplicability of guidance related to forced reactor coolant flow and loops, thus connecting their discussion of coolant flow mechanisms.

Both Section A and Section B explain why certain regulatory guidance related to coolant flow and auxiliary feedwater systems for PWRs is not applicable to the NuScale design, specifically because NuScale does not use reactor coolant pumps or traditional AFW systems, which directly affects the flow of coolant between the steam generator and the reactor.

Both Section A and Section B explain why certain regulatory guidelines related to coolant flow and auxiliary feedwater systems are not applicable to the NuScale design due to its lack of reactor coolant pumps and use of a different heat removal system, thus providing context for how coolant flows between the steam generator and reactor in this specific design.

Both Section A and Section B state that the NuScale design does not require or include reactor coolant pumps, connecting them through their shared explanation that guidance referring to forced reactor coolant flow and loops is not applicable.

Both Section A and Section B state that the NuScale design does not require or include reactor coolant pumps, connecting them through the shared information that forced reactor coolant flow and associated loops/pumps are not applicable to the NuScale system.

Section A discusses design guidelines for preheat steam generator designs, while Section B addresses once-through steam generator designs, both referencing the avoidance of water hammers and the role of auxiliary feedwater supply in the context of NuScale's conformance with regulatory criteria, thus specifying different technical details for related coolant flow systems.

Section A discusses design guidelines and system functions related to coolant flow and auxiliary systems in the NuScale reactor, while Section B addresses regulatory criteria for reactor coolant system integrity and notes the inapplicability of certain requirements due to NuScale's unique coolant flow design without pumps, thus both sections are connected by their discussion of how coolant flows between the steam generator and reactor in the context of NuScale's design.

Section A discusses design guidelines and system functions related to coolant flow and auxiliary systems in the NuScale reactor, while Section B addresses regulatory requirements and safety analysis for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures, both referencing the NuScale design's unique approach to coolant flow without traditional pumps, thus connecting through their discussion of system design and regulatory conformance regarding coolant flow.

Section A discusses design guidelines and system functions related to auxiliary feedwater and steam generator operation, while Section B addresses safety analysis criteria for reactor coolant system behavior, both referencing the NuScale design's lack of traditional reactor coolant loops and pumps, thus connecting through the design's unique coolant flow configuration.

Section A describes design guidelines and system functions related to auxiliary feedwater and steam generator operation, while Section B outlines regulatory requirements and safety analysis criteria for reactor coolant and main steam system pressures; both are connected through their focus on the flow and control of coolant between the steam generator and reactor, specifically addressing how the NuScale design conforms to or differs from traditional PWR requirements.

Section A and Section B both discuss the applicability of regulatory guidance for PWR designs with forced coolant flow to the NuScale design, specifically noting that NuScale does not use reactor coolant pumps, which affects both fuel cladding integrity (A) and system pressures (B) in the context of coolant flow between the steam generator and reactor.

Section A describes the physical configuration and flow path of the reactor coolant system, while Section B explains that certain regulatory requirements related to coolant loop startup and flow control are not applicable to the NuScale design due to its lack of reactor coolant pumps, directly connecting the design features in A to the regulatory applicability in B.

Section A describes the physical configuration and flow path of the reactor core and coolant system, while Section B explains that certain regulatory requirements for coolant loop startup and flow control are not applicable to the NuScale design due to its lack of reactor coolant pumps and traditional loops, thus connecting the design features in A to the regulatory context in B.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor and cooling water system layout, while Section B discusses regulatory guidelines for steam generator design and clarifies that certain auxiliary feedwater system requirements do not apply to NuScale's design, connecting them through their focus on the systems involved in coolant flow between the steam generator and reactor.

Section A describes the passive, natural circulation coolant flow between the steam generator and reactor in the NuScale design, while Section B explains that regulatory criteria related to forced coolant flow and pumps are not applicable because NuScale uses natural circulation as described in Section A.

Section B explains that the regulatory guidance for forced-flow reactor coolant systems does not apply to the NuScale design, while Section A describes the NuScale system's use of natural circulation for coolant flow between the steam generator and reactor, thus providing the technical basis for the inapplicability stated in Section B.

Section A describes the NuScale plant's decay heat removal system and its operation in cooling the reactor coolant system via the steam generator, while Section B references regulatory guidelines for auxiliary feedwater systems and clarifies that NuScale's DHRS serves a similar function, connecting them through the functional equivalence of DHRS to AFW in the context of coolant flow between the steam generator and reactor.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor core and its connection to the hot leg riser, while Section B details the closed-loop cooling system and its interaction with the steam generator (SG), together describing the flow path and cooling mechanisms between the reactor and SG.

Section A provides regulatory criteria and conformance information for systems involved in coolant flow (such as steam and feedwater systems), while Section B explains why certain regulatory guidance on coolant loop startup and flow control is not applicable to the NuScale design due to its lack of reactor coolant pumps and loops, thus connecting them through the design features that determine coolant flow paths.

Section A describes regulatory criteria and conformance for systems involved in coolant flow (such as steam and feedwater systems), while Section B explains why certain regulatory guidance on coolant loop startup and flow control is not applicable to the NuScale design due to the absence of reactor coolant pumps, thus connecting them through the design's unique coolant flow configuration.

Section A references regulatory criteria for the steam and feedwater systems relevant to coolant flow, while Section B provides specific design guidelines (such as avoiding water hammers) that must be considered in the design and operation of the steam generator and its connection to the reactor, thus Section B specifies technical details that support the general regulatory framework outlined in Section A.
